items.acceptanceCriteria: Equipment documentation is available
Previous inspection reports are checked
Daily pre-operational inspection checklists are checked (if applicable)
Preventive Maintenance is established and updated records are maintained
Rated load is clearly marked on both sides of crane bridge (Arabic & English)
Rated load is clearly marked on hoist or trolley unit
Crane is painted safety yellow
Crane is painted safety yellow-and-black stripes on offshore facilities
A sign is posted warning the operator not to rely solely on any automatic device as a substitute for safe operating practice
Crane manufacturer name, address, serial number and power ratings are clearly marked or tagged
Precautionary warnings to operator are clearly marked
Clearance exits between the crane and sides of the building or adjacent crane are maintained throughout all motions
Controls and protective equipment are within reach of the operator inside the cab
The hook block is visible from operator station at all times
Cab is attached to the crane to minimize swaying and vibrations
Access to the cab or bridge walkway is by a fixed ladder, stairs, or platform
The clearance from the surface of the platform to the nearest overhead obstruction is 1220 mm (48")
The service platform width is at least 457 mm (18") except at the bridge mechanism where it is not less than 380 mm (15")
The electrical control cabinet door(s) are opening 90 degree or removable type
Service platform walking surface is slip-resistant
Service platform is provided with guard railings and toeboards
Emergency escape is possible from the cab
Stairways are non-slip and have a maximum incline angle of 50 degree
Each hoisting unit is equipped with at least one holding brake
The holding brake is applied to the motor shaft or a gear reducer shaft
The holding brake torque rating is not less than the percentage of rated load hoisting torque at the point where the brake is applied (based on the crane design)
Pendant control cable is properly enclosed, grounded and suspended with a separate support cable
Pendant control push-button enclosure is marked for identification of functions
Electrical equipment is guarded and not exposed to oil, moisture, dirt and inadvertent contact
Audio warning device(s) are fitted (one or more of the following: Gong, Bell/Siren/Horn, Rotating Beacon and/or strope light)
Lifting and lowering functional test is satisfactory
Crane trolley travel functional test is satisfactory
Crane bridge travel functional test is satisfactory
Hoist limit device functional test is satisfactory
Crane Travel limit device functional test is satisfactory
Wire rope end connections do not have corrosion
Ropes are correctly lubricated

Removal criteria for rope replacement shall be as follows: • In running ropes, 12 randomly broken wires in 1 lay or 4 broken wires in 1 strand of 1 lay. • 1 broken wire protruding from the core • Reductions from nominal rope diameter greater than 5% • Kinking, crushing, birdcaging or other distortion.
Wire rope is not corroded
The rope is adequately lubricated
A wind speed indicating device is provided for cranes used outdoors
Fire extinguisher installed in the cab.(10BC minimum rated)

HOISTING COMPONENTS 2.1.1 Hoist rope is guarded from chafing where required 2.1.2 Hook(s) can rotate freely Sections in contact with saddles, equalizer sheaves, or other sheaves 2.1.3 where rope travel is limited are not rapid deterioration. 2.1.4 Surface condition of rope drum(s) show no defects and are smooth Sheave grooves shall be free from surface defects which could cause 2.1.5 rope damage Sheaves carrying ropes, which can be momentarily unloaded, are provided with close-fitting guards, or other devices, to guide the rope 2.1.6 back into the groove when the load is reapplied. 2.1.7 Rope construction is as per manufacturer recommendations 2.1.8 Lower hoist limit cut-out (if fitted) is properly working

TROLLEY COMPONENTS 2.2.1 Stops and bumpers are fitted to each end of the trolley(s) Trolley truck rail sweeps are provided in front of the leading wheels on 2.2.2 both ends of the trolley end truck Clearance between the top surface of the rail head and the bottom of 2.2.3 the sweep does not exceed 3Ú16" (5 mm) The sweep extends below the top surface of the rail head, for a distance not less than 50% of the thickness of the rail head, on both sides of the 2.2.4 rail head Clearance between the side surface of the rail head and the side of the sweep which extends below the top surface of the rail head is equal to 2.2.5 crane float plus 3Ú16" (5 mm) 2.2.6 Trolley(s) brakes are operable Trolley unit of the crane equipped with either a braking means or have 2.2.7 trolley drive frictional characteristics Trolley travel warnings (e.g. gong, beacon, bell or strope light) are 2.2.8 operable 2.2.9 Unusual sounds are not present during trolley travel 2.2.10 Trolley has no missing or loose parts 2.2.11 Trolley wheels have no sign of excessive wear 2.2.12 Chain drive and sprocket have no wear or stretch 2.2.13 All moving parts are correctly lubricated

BRIDGE COMPONENTS Crane Bridge stops within stipulated 10% distance of rated load speed 2.3.1 under frictional forces (if no braking means provided) 2.3.2 Bridge brakes comply with crane design requirements 2.3.3 Trolley truck frame drop is limited to 25mm
CABIN 2.4.1 Safety labels are displayed and legible 2.4.2 Integral outside platform is in place and door opens outward or slides 2.4.3 Trapdoor has a clear opening of not less than 610mm

BRIDGE STRUCTURE 2.5.1 Welded structures and members do not have cracks or corrosion Adequate clearances exists between two parallel crane bridges (if there 2.5.2 are no intervening walls or structures) 2.5.3 Minimum working space on service platforms is 1220mm (48") 2.5.4 Minimum passageway on service platform is 457mm (18") 2.5.5 Doors of electrical cabinets to open 90 degrees or be removable

items.acceptanceCriteria: ELECTRICALS

HOOKS 2.7.1 Labeling and manufacturer data are available and legible 2.7.2 Safe working load is clearly marked on the hook Hook is not bent or twisted 2.7.3 Hook is not distorted in the throat opening 2.7.4 • Max. allowable throat opening is 5% not to exceed 1Ú4 in. (6 mm) or as per manufacturer recommendations Maximum wear in the hook bowl is not exceeding 10% (compared to 2.7.5 new hook) or as per manufacturer recommendations 2.7.6 Hook is not cracked, gouged or shows nicks 2.7.7 Hook can lock (if it is a self-locking hook)
